political.s: WellCare and parent Centene showed mixed political leanings with $576K in donations. As a private subsidiary post-2020 acquisition, recent political activity is less transparent. Historical 2018 lobbying exceeded $3M annually.

charity.s: WellCare demonstrated strong charitable commitment through Civic 50 recognition (multiple years) and WAVE employee volunteer program. Community Foundation supports underserved populations with focus on health and social determinants.
charity.focus: Healthcare access, community health, underserved populations

environment.s: Limited publicly available environmental reporting. As a healthcare insurance company, primary environmental impact is operational. No comprehensive sustainability commitments disclosed.

labor.s: Mixed employee reviews on Glassdoor (3.6/5 rating). Reports of benefits and team culture, but concerns about micromanagement, limited advancement, and pay equity. Some wage/hour disputes documented.

dei.s: WellCare demonstrates pro-DEI stance with dedicated Chief Public Affairs Officer overseeing diversity initiatives and explicit commitment to serving diverse populations. Leadership emphasizes culturally responsive care.

privacy.s: WellCare complies with HIPAA and maintains privacy notice requirements. Shows awareness of data sharing risks through CARIN Code of Conduct references, but privacy practices vary across subsidiaries.

execPay.s: Significant pay disparity: prior CEO (2018) earned $12.7M while median employee earned approximately $70K-90K, suggesting 140-180:1 ratio. Parent company Centene CEO-to-worker ratio documented at 224:1 (2024).

fec.committeeName: WELLCARE HEALTH PLANS, INC. PAC (WELLCARE PAC)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
